transcript:
HCP: right, so what brings you in today?
Patient: well, i've been having these really bad headaches for a few weeks now
HCP: where exactly is the pain located?
Patient: mostly on the left side, behind my eye
HCP: does it get worse with movement?
Patient: yes, especially if i bend over or cough
HCP: okay, have you had any recent head injuries or changes in your vision?
Patient: no, i haven't had any falls but my vision does seem a bit blurry sometimes
HCP: i see, and how is your blood pressure?
Patient: i don't really know, i haven't checked recently
HCP: we should check that before we refer you
Patient: sure, i'll get the machine
HCP: actually, i'll do it now if i can
Patient: ok, that would be good
HCP: now i'm thinking we need to refer you to a neurologist to look at this properly
Patient: oh really, is it something serious
HCP: i don't want to rule out anything like a tumour or aneurysm
Patient: right, i understand
HCP: i'm also worried about the blurry vision you mentioned
Patient: yeah, it comes and goes
HCP: i'll write to Neurology and ask them to review your case and maybe do some scans
Patient: thanks doc, i appreciate it
HCP: no worries, i'll also give you some panadol for the pain while we wait
Patient: okay, thanks

reason.text: headaches on left side behind eye
reason.evidence_span: having these really bad headaches for a few weeks now

history.evidence_span: my vision does seem a bit blurry sometimes

request.text: review your case and maybe do some scans
request.evidence_span: ask them to review your case and maybe do some scans

transcript:
HCP: right, so what brings you in today?
Patient: well, i've been having these sharp pains in my knee for about two weeks now
HCP: where exactly on the knee?
Patient: more like the front part, below the kneecap
HCP: and any swelling or clicking sounds?
Patient: there's a bit of swelling and it clicks when i bend it
HCP: i think we should refer you to Orthopaedics for a proper assessment
Patient: okay, thanks
HCP: yes, i'll need to see what you've been taking for pain
Patient: i've been using panadol and some ice packs
HCP: got it, panadol regularly, i'll mention that in the letter
Patient: thanks doctor
HCP: no problem, i'll also ask them to check your x-rays before any surgery

reason.text: sharp pains in front of knee with swelling and clicking
reason.evidence_span: sharp pains in my knee for about two weeks now

history.evidence_span: there's a bit of swelling and it clicks when i bend it

request.text: assessment and check x-rays before surgery
request.evidence_span: ask them to check your x-rays before any surgery

transcript:
HCP: hi karen, how are you feeling today?
Patient: well, better than last week, just a bit of tummy upset really
HCP: right, sounds like the gastroenterology visit is the main thing we need to sort out
Patient: yeah, the bloating has been terrible after meals
HCP: and you've been taking omeprazole twice a day for the last two months?
Patient: yes, but it hasn't quite cleared it up
HCP: okay, I'll write to Gastroenterology to request a colonoscopy and review your symptoms

transcript:
HCP: thanks for coming in, i know you had a bit of a rough night
Patient: yeah, i was up all night with the coughing and the tightness
HCP: right, sounds like your asthma is acting up again, we need to get you to Respiratory
Patient: oh, is it serious? i'm on ventolin but it's not helping much
HCP: not necessarily serious, but with the history of smoke exposure i want to rule out other things
Patient: i've been smoking for twenty years, trying to quit now
HCP: good effort, and please keep taking your salbutamol as prescribed while we sort this out

HCP: so you want to get a referral for your knee?
Patient: yeah, it's been hurting a lot lately
HCP: specifically what kind of pain do you feel when you walk?
Patient: it feels like sharp pain in the front of the knee
HCP: and how long has this been going on for?
Patient: about a month now, started after I went for a run
HCP: have you tried any pain relief yet?
Patient: i've been taking panadol but it's not helping much
HCP: okay, i think we should refer you to Orthopaedics for a proper assessment
Patient: thanks doc, that sounds good
HCP: yes, i'll note down the pain on movement and the one month duration in the referral

reason.text: sharp pain in front of knee on walking
reason.evidence_span: sharp pain in the front of the knee

history.text: pain for about a month after running
history.evidence_span: about a month now, started after I went for a run

request.evidence_span: refer you to Orthopaedics for a proper assessment

transcript:
HCP: right so what brings you in today?
Patient: well um i've been feeling really tired lately and short of breath when i climb stairs
HCP: that sounds tiring indeed, how long has this been going on?
Patient: about two months i guess, started getting worse after i moved house
HCP: moving house is a big deal, did you notice any swelling in your legs too?
Patient: yes actually my ankles feel puffy especially in the evenings
HCP: right let's check your blood pressure then, okay so you take amlodipine for this
Patient: yeah um one tablet in the morning and sometimes i add some panadol if my head hurts
HCP: good to know, and i also want to refer you to a cardio specialist for further testing
Patient: oh right um is it serious or just a check up?
HCP: we need to be careful, the combination of breathlessness and ankle swelling needs proper investigation
Patient: ok thanks doctor i'll wait for the letter then
HCP: definitely, i'll write to the specialist and ask them to see you soon for an echo

reason.text: tiredness shortness of breath ankle swelling
reason.evidence_span: feeling really tired lately and short of breath when i climb stairs

history.evidence_span: notice any swelling in your legs too

take amlodipine for this

sometimes i add some panadol if my head hurts

transcript:
HCP: so i have a referral for you to see an orthopaedic surgeon
Patient: okay what did you find with my knee
HCP: your pain is getting worse with walking and you have a swollen joint
Patient: yeah it has been puffy all week and walking hurts
HCP: that sounds like a mechanical issue we need to rule out so i will ask for an x-ray
Patient: i am taking ibuprofen for the pain
HCP: yes panadol and ibuprofen but the swelling needs proper assessment
Patient: right i will go along
HCP: i will send the form to Orthopaedics and ask for a clinical review and x-ray

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for coming in today
Patient: yeah, thanks for seeing me
HCP: so what brings you in, any specific issues?
Patient: well, i've been having a lot of knee pain lately, especially when walking up stairs
HCP: i see, and where exactly is the pain located?
Patient: mostly on the front part of the knee, it gets worse after a walk
HCP: right, have you tried any treatments so far?
Patient: i've been taking panadol and doing some ice packs, but it hasn't helped much
HCP: okay, let me check your knee now, just a quick exam
Patient: sure
HCP: hmm, there's some swelling and tenderness here
Patient: oh, that's what i was worried about
HCP: don't worry, we'll get you sorted, i think we need to refer you to an orthopaedic specialist
Patient: okay, will that help?
HCP: yes, they can do better imaging and check for any structural issues
Patient: great, thanks for explaining
HCP: no problem, i'll also note down your current meds and history for them
Patient: thanks, i'm glad to finally get some help

specialty.evidence_span: refer you to an orthopaedic specialist

reason.text: knee pain on walking up stairs with swelling
reason.evidence_span: having a lot of knee pain lately, especially when walking up stairs

history.text: pain on the front part of the knee after a walk
history.evidence_span: mostly on the front part of the knee, it gets worse after a walk

history.text: swelling and tenderness on knee exam
history.evidence_span: there's some swelling and tenderness here

request.text: better imaging and check for structural issues
request.evidence_span: they can do better imaging and check for any structural issues

transcript:
HCP: good morning, thanks for coming in
Patient: hi doctor, just want to get some help with my knee
HCP: yes, you've been limping for a few weeks now, is that right
Patient: yeah, it started after i played tennis on the weekend
HCP: i see, and it hurts when you go down stairs or bend to pick things up
Patient: exactly, especially the right knee
HCP: based on your history of previous meniscus surgery i want to be cautious
Patient: oh right, that was five years ago
HCP: yes, and you're taking ibuprofen for pain but it's not helping much
Patient: correct, 400mg twice a day
HCP: ok so i think we need to refer you to Orthopaedics for a specialist review and possible MRI
Patient: thanks doctor, appreciate the help

reason.text: right knee pain after tennis and bending

current_meds.evidence_span: taking ibuprofen for pain but it's not helping much

request.evidence_span: refer you to Orthopaedics for a specialist review and possible MRI
